manifest_release_notes: APK split per ABI + native download engine + event-driven sync

summary.en: Per-ABI APK + native download engine + event-driven sync

notes_markdown.es:
# Kumoriya v0.3.0 — Notas de Versión Alpha

**Fecha:** 2026-04-24

## Resumen

Versión enfocada en descargas y distribución: un motor de descargas nativo reescrito para Android, sincronización local/servidor event-driven, y un pipeline de release que parte el APK por arquitectura para que descargues solo lo que tu dispositivo necesita.

## Agregado

- **APK dividido por ABI** — ahora publicamos APKs separados para `arm64-v8a`, `armeabi-v7a` y `x86_64`, más un `universal` como fallback. El actualizador dentro de la app detecta tu arquitectura automáticamente con `device_info_plus` y descarga el split correcto (~35–45 MB) en lugar del universal completo (~90 MB). El manifest (`/releases/latest`) y `update.json` ahora exponen `universal` y un mapa `abis.*` con `size_bytes` y `sha256` por artefacto.
- **Motor nativo de descargas reescrito (Android)** — `kumoriya_exoplayer` incluye un engine standalone basado en OkHttp + coroutines que reemplaza el `DownloadManager` de Media3. MP4/WebM/MKV directos vía un `ParallelDownloader` consciente de `Range`, HLS por fetch de segmentos más remux opcional a MP4 con Media3 `Transformer` (con fallback a concat `.ts` si el remux falla o falta disco).
- **Cola de descargas con límite de concurrencia** — como máximo tres descargas simultáneas; el resto espera en cola FIFO como `PENDING` y se promueve automáticamente al liberarse slots.
- **Auto-reanudación al reinicio en frío** — tareas que quedaron en `downloading`, `pending`, `remuxing` o `disconnected` cuando murió el proceso se re-encolan al siguiente arranque y continúan desde `.state.json` + `.partial` sin volver a bajar lo ya descargado.
- **Descargas sobreviven al cierre / swipe** — engine, centro de notificaciones y sink de eventos son ahora process-scope; `FOREGROUND_SERVICE_DATA_SYNC` + `stopWithTask="false"` mantienen las descargas activas al quitar la app de recientes.
- **Taxonomía de errores + retry** — `DownloadErrorClassifier` clasifica fallos en `FailFast` / `RetryOnce` / `RetryBackoff` / `StorageFull` / `Disconnected` con backoff 1/2/4/8/16s (tope 5). 5xx y timeouts reintentan, 404 reintenta una vez, 401/403/410 fallan rápido. Errores terminales llevan ahora un `errorCode` estable (`download.storage_full`, `download.server_error_503`, etc.).
- **WiFi-only con auto-resume** — al perder conectividad o activar "solo WiFi", las tareas activas y encoladas se pausan automáticamente como `DISCONNECTED` y se reanudan cuando vuelve una red aceptable.
- **Notificaciones por tarea + resumen agrupado** — cada descarga activa tiene su propia notificación con Pausar/Reanudar/Cancelar; un resumen agrupado ofrece Pausar todo / Reanudar todo.
- **Sincronización event-driven** — el nuevo `SyncCoordinator` orquesta seis triggers (escritura local con debounce 500 ms, resume del ciclo de vida si pasaron > 6 h, reconexión de red, `paused` con `OneTimeWorkRequest` en Android, flush bloqueante pre-logout de 5 s, y tarea periódica Workmanager cada 12 h).
- **Pull independiente del push** — `SyncCoordinator.triggerPull()` llama solo `pullSince(lastSyncAt)` con lock propio; cada resume hace pull (debounce 30 s) y mientras la app está abierta se dispara pull cada 30 min para ver cambios de otros dispositivos del mismo usuario.

## Cambios

- **`media_kit_libs_video` reemplazado por libs desktop-only** — el APK ya no incluye `libmpv / libavcodec / libdav1d` (~30–50 MB de `.so` menos). La reproducción en Android va exclusivamente por `kumoriya_exoplayer` (Media3); `MediaKit.ensureInitialized()` se gatea con `!Platform.isAndroid`.
- **Contrato `DownloadBackend`** — una única abstracción enruta Android al `NativeDownloadBackend` y desktop al `DartDownloadBackend` (envuelve al `DownloadManagerService` existente). Los casos de uso y la UI dependen del contrato, no de backends concretos.
- **Stack HTTP unificado** — reproductor y downloader ahora comparten `KumoriyaHttpClient` (singleton OkHttp con pool, redirects, timeouts y extracción de UA). La reproducción Media3 usa `OkHttpDataSource.Factory` en lugar de `DefaultHttpDataSource`.

## Corregido

- **UI de descargas no se actualizaba** — el flujo nativo ahora emite eventos `state` explícitos (pending → downloading → completed/failed) y el servicio Dart los refleja inmediatamente en Drift y en los streams de Riverpod.
- **Descargas HLS atascadas** — Media3 bajaba solo el `.m3u8` al tratar todos los streams como progressive; ahora las URLs HLS van por `HlsDownloadPreparer` con el MIME correcto.
- **Eventos tempranos perdidos** — el sink del `EventChannel` ahora es mutable con buffer; los eventos previos a la suscripción de Flutter se drenan al adjuntarse.
- **Cancelar dejaba descargas como `failed`** — cancelar ahora borra el task de Drift y limpia segmentos/caché temporales (cancel = delete).
- **Headers custom duplicados en la URL** al entregar descargas al plugin nativo.
- **Cola atascada tras cold start** — sin el reconcile pass, tareas `pending`/`downloading` en Drift no tenían job nativo; pause/resume no hacían nada.
- **Swipe-away mataba descargas** — el detach de Flutter destruía el engine y paraba el FGS. El engine pasó a singleton process-scope (`DownloadCore`).
- **`.ts` y `.mp4` huérfanos al cancelar** — el path de cancel ahora barre ambas variantes del remux HLS más sus `.partial`.
- **Auto-pause perdía tareas en race de enqueue** — la cola de espera ahora está lockeada para evitar over-count; los clicks duplicados en resume/retry deduplican por `taskId`.

## Notas de Migración

- Ninguna para usuarios finales. Si tu dispositivo cae fuera de las tres ABIs soportadas, el actualizador descarga el APK `universal`.
- Backend: esta versión añade la tabla `app_release_android_artifacts` con backfill del universal legacy; retrocompatible con el shape anterior.

## Plataformas

- Android (APK por ABI + universal)
- Windows (publicación separada si el instalador de esta versión aún no está disponible)
notes_markdown.en:
# Kumoriya v0.3.0 — Alpha Release Notes

**Date:** 2026-04-24

## Summary

Release focused on downloads and distribution: a brand-new native download engine for Android, event-driven local/server sync, and a release pipeline that splits the APK per architecture so you only download what your device needs.

## Added

- **Android APK split per ABI** — we now publish separate APKs for `arm64-v8a`, `armeabi-v7a`, and `x86_64`, plus a `universal` fallback. The in-app updater auto-detects your architecture with `device_info_plus` and downloads the matching split (~35–45 MB) instead of the ~90 MB universal APK. The manifest (`/releases/latest`) and `update.json` now expose `universal` and a per-ABI `abis.*` map with `size_bytes` and `sha256` for integrity checks.
- **Native download engine rebuilt from scratch (Android)** — `kumoriya_exoplayer` ships a standalone OkHttp + coroutines download engine that replaces the legacy Media3 `DownloadManager` path. Direct MP4/WebM/MKV via a range-aware `ParallelDownloader`, HLS via segment fetch plus optional Media3 `Transformer` remux to MP4 (with `.ts` concat fallback on failure or low disk).
- **Download queue with concurrency cap** — at most three downloads run in parallel; the rest wait as `PENDING` in a FIFO queue and are promoted automatically as slots free up.
- **Auto-resume on cold start** — tasks that were `downloading`, `pending`, `remuxing`, or `disconnected` when the process died are re-enqueued on next launch and resume from `.state.json` + `.partial` bytes without re-downloading completed segments.
- **Downloads survive app close / swipe-away** — engine, notification center, and event sink are now process-scope; `FOREGROUND_SERVICE_DATA_SYNC` + `stopWithTask="false"` keep downloads running after the user swipes the app from recents.
- **Retry + error taxonomy** — `DownloadErrorClassifier` maps failures into `FailFast` / `RetryOnce` / `RetryBackoff` / `StorageFull` / `Disconnected`. 5xx and timeouts retry 1/2/4/8/16s (cap 5); 404 retries once; 401/403/410 fail fast. Terminal failures now carry a stable `errorCode` (`download.storage_full`, `download.server_error_503`, etc.).
- **WiFi-only + auto-resume on reconnect** — toggling "WiFi only" or losing connectivity auto-pauses active + queued tasks as `DISCONNECTED`; they resume automatically when an acceptable network returns.
- **Per-task + grouped download notifications** — each active download has its own notification with Pause/Resume/Cancel actions; a grouped summary exposes Pause all / Resume all.
- **Event-driven sync coordinator** — the new `SyncCoordinator` orchestrates six triggers: local writes with a 500 ms debounce, `AppLifecycleState.resumed` (full sync if the last sync is > 6 h old), network reconnection, `paused` scheduling a `OneTimeWorkRequest` on Android, blocking flush pre-logout (5 s timeout), and a periodic Workmanager task every 12 h as a fallback.
- **Pull independent of push** — new `SyncCoordinator.triggerPull()` calls only `pullSince(lastSyncAt)` under its own lock. Each foreground resume now pulls (30 s debounce), and a 30-min timer keeps pulling while the app is open so changes from other devices appear without waiting for the next close/open cycle.

## Changed

- **`media_kit_libs_video` replaced with desktop-only libs** — the Android APK no longer ships `libmpv / libavcodec / libdav1d` (~30–50 MB of `.so` saved). Android playback runs exclusively through `kumoriya_exoplayer` (Media3); `MediaKit.ensureInitialized()` is gated with `!Platform.isAndroid`.
- **`DownloadBackend` contract** — a single abstraction routes Android to `NativeDownloadBackend` and desktop to `DartDownloadBackend` (wraps the existing `DownloadManagerService`). Use cases and UI depend on the contract, not on concrete backends.
- **Unified HTTP stack** — player and downloader now share `KumoriyaHttpClient` (single OkHttp client with pool, redirects, timeouts, and UA extraction). Media3 playback uses `OkHttpDataSource.Factory` instead of `DefaultHttpDataSource`.

## Fixed

- **Downloads UI not updating** — the native flow now emits explicit `state` events (pending → downloading → completed/failed) and the Dart service reflects them immediately in Drift and Riverpod streams.
- **HLS downloads getting stuck** — Media3 was treating every stream as progressive and downloading only the `.m3u8`; HLS URLs now go through `HlsDownloadPreparer` with the correct MIME.
- **Early events lost** — the `EventChannel` sink is now mutable and buffered; events occurring before Flutter subscribes are drained on attach.
- **Cancel left downloads marked `failed`** — cancel now deletes the task from Drift and cleans up segments / temp cache (cancel = delete).
- **Duplicated custom headers in URL** when handing a download to the native plugin.
- **Queue stuck after cold start** — before the reconciliation pass, `pending`/`downloading` Drift rows had no matching native job; pause/resume were no-ops.
- **Swipe-away killed downloads** — Flutter detach used to destroy the engine and stop the FGS. Engine ownership moved to a process-scope singleton (`DownloadCore`).
- **`.ts` and `.mp4` artifacts orphaned on cancel** — the cancel path now sweeps both HLS remux variants plus their `.partial` siblings.
- **Auto-pause lost task on enqueue race** — the waiting queue is locked so concurrent enqueues cannot over-count active jobs; duplicate resume/retry clicks dedupe on `taskId`.

## Migration Notes

- None for end users. If your device's ABI is not one of the three supported, the updater falls back to the `universal` APK.
- Backend: this version adds the `app_release_android_artifacts` table with a backfill from the legacy universal column; backward-compatible with the previous shape.

## Platforms

- Android (per-ABI APK + universal)
- Windows (published separately if the installer for this version is not yet available)
